commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an exec denylist mode across TypeScript and macOS exec approval evaluation, config/schema/CLI/UI/docs, logging, and regression tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction. For the PR behavior, the body provides redacted live gateway logs and screenshots, and source inspection confirms denylist evaluation happens before execution.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is human maintainer/security acceptance of the new exec policy contract, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: Feature/exec denylist
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: This PR introduces a new feature - exec denylist. this is done to bridge the gap between unusable allowlist where the agent asks approval for every single command it runs and full on YOLO agent can do everything unrestricted. The feature sets some boundary for the agent so we don't have agent bypass things like web_search tool and run curl/wget instead and get prompt injected. - Problem: - Why it matters: safer than YOLO - What changed: added exec denylist feature - What did NOT change (scope boundary): other features ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [x] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior addressed: exec denylist blocks model-initiated shell network fetches (`curl`/`wget`) in a real gateway run, preventing a headless agent from bypassing safer fetch tooling. - Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway and Control UI exec approvals on macOS, running an Ollama `qwen3.6:35b-a3b-q4_K_M` scheduled cron agent with effective `tools.exec` security resolved to `denylist`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Configured exec approvals to use denylist, ran the scheduled `AI Lab Morning Brief` cron job, then inspected live gateway logs and the persisted runtime transcript with `openclaw logs --limit 300 --plain` and the matching session transcript. - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Screenshots above plus copied redacted live runtime output below. - Observed result after fix: Seven attempted `exec` commands containing `curl` were denied by rule `default-shell-network-fetch`; the model received `exec command is denied due to command in deny list`, then switched to `web_fetch` and completed the cron run instead of bypassing the safer fetch path. - What was not tested: content:notTested - This proof did not verify iMessage delivery; the cron delivery later timed out on the local iMessage bridge, unrelated to exec denylist behavior. It also did not test every possible custom deny rule shape beyond the default shell network-fetch rule. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Redacted runtime evidence: ```text 2026-05-17T11:15:14.803Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=f65c003a792cfdc0 length=100 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-17T11:15:14.804Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=16c1f70c7f7d90e6 length=655 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-17T11:15:14.805Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=10a2a034cfd89748 length=125 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-17T11:15:14.806Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=72a121ccd1eeb9f2 length=126 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-17T11:15:14.807Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=a0ea71b5b6367cd0 length=169 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-17T11:15:14.808Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=1b2a1a8991ec2015 length=123 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-17T11:15:14.809Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=cc66320e783f16f7 length=527 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 redacted transcript: toolResult x7 -> exec command is denied due to command in deny list redacted transcript: assistant -> Exec is blocked on this run. Let me use web_fetch for the heavy lifting. cron result: finished status=ok durationMs=90606 model=qwen3.6:35b-a3b-q4_K_M provider=ollama ``` Most recent redacted exec denylist runtime logs (captured after the latest branch cleanup): ```text command: pnpm openclaw logs --limit 500 --plain | rg -i "exec denylist|denylist|SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED|deny list" | tail -80 2026-05-19T11:15:09.431Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=297ca013654e79a0 length=165 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-19T11:15:09.434Z warn exec denylist: denied command hash=297ca013654e79a0 length=165 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-20T02:42:36.290Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=58b881f7a4e5588a length=45 host=gateway agent=main trigger=user ruleIndex=0 2026-05-20T02:42:36.291Z warn exec denylist: denied command hash=58b881f7a4e5588a length=45 host=gateway agent=main trigger=user ruleIndex=0 ``` Observed result after latest runtime check: the gateway denied both cron-triggered and user-triggered exec attempts through denylist rule index 0 without logging raw command text; logs contain hashes, lengths, host, agent, trigger, and rule index only. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) Tests were written for this, but for validation so deny mode is synthetic ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Denies undesired exec runs List user-visible changes (including defaults/config). Denylist is now an option in exec security configuration: Control UI -> Nodes -> Exec approvals -> ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: This actually shrinks the YOLO execution surface... ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: MacOS Tahoe Version 26.4.1 (25E253) - Runtime/container: node - Model/provider: Qwen 3.6 35b/ollama - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): denylist ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: see screenshots - Edge cases checked: not sure what edge case i would need for a deny list - What you did **not** verify: other features ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. - Shadow told me to do it... If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes`) - Config/env changes? (`Yes`) - Migration needed? (`No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: None - Mitigation: None
target.author: juanhuaxu
